g.data-narration: Welcome to Android Environment Configuration - the essential first step to becoming an Android developer.
Setting up Android Studio and SDK creates your development workspace for writing, testing, and building mobile applications.
g.text: A proper development environment is essential for
writing code, testing apps, and debugging issues.
This setup is your workspace for the entire course.

g.data-narration: Your computer needs Windows 7+, 4GB RAM minimum (8GB recommended), 4GB+ disk space, and 1280x800 resolution.

svg.g:
System Requirements
OS: Win/Mac/Linux 64-bit
RAM: 4GB min, 8GB rec.
<g transform="translate("390, 200)">
Disk: 4GB+ free space

g.data-narration: Android Studio comes with an embedded JDK, but understanding Java is crucial since Android apps run on Java libraries.
g.text: Android Studio includes embedded JDK - usually no separate installation needed.

g.data-narration: Download Android Studio from developer.android.com/studio and run the installer for your operating system.
The SDK Manager provides tools, platforms, and build libraries needed to create Android applications.
g.text: Access: Tools → SDK Manager in Android Studio

g.data-narration: SDK includes platform tools, build tools, Android platforms (API levels), and system images for emulators.
The AVD Manager creates virtual Android devices (emulators) for testing apps without a physical phone.
g.text: Access: Tools → AVD Manager in Android Studio

g.data-narration: Create AVD by selecting device definition, choosing system image, configuring name and orientation, then finish.
g.text: System image downloads automatically if not present. Choose API level matching targetSdkVersion.

g.data-narration: Gradle automates building, compiles code, manages dependencies, and packages everything into an APK file.
The build.gradle file configures compileSdkVersion, minSdkVersion, targetSdkVersion, applicationId, and dependencies.
g.text: Key SDK versions: compileSdk (build), minSdk (minimum support), targetSdk (tested version)

g.data-narration: Create a new project: select Empty Activity template, configure app name, package, language Kotlin/Java, then build and run.
Key takeaways: Android Studio IDE, SDK Manager for tools/platforms, AVD for emulators, Gradle builds APK files.
Remember: SDK versions in build.gradle control compatibility. Validate setup by running Hello World on emulator.
Your Android environment is now ready! Use this workspace throughout the course to build apps, learn coding, and create projects.
g.text: Run Hello World to verify setup → Start building amazing apps!
